pattern: A tiny numeric range makes direct enumeration with a digit-sum split the simplest correct approach.
hints: Odd-length numbers can never be symmetric, so you only need to look at even-length ones.
For an even-length number, split its digits exactly in half and compare the two halves' digit sums.
Iterate every integer from low to high, skip odd digit-lengths, and count the ones whose first-half digit sum equals its second-half digit sum.

edge_inputs.description: range of only single-digit odd-length numbers
range spanning a digit-length change from two to four digits

stress.description: random sub-ranges across the full bound
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    low = rng.randint(1, 9999)
    high = rng.randint(low, 9999)
    return [low, high]
